summary: Replaced progress.yaml writes with append-only summary.jsonl, updated summary loading in runner/app paths, and added JSONL regression coverage. Verification passed: go test ./..., go build ./..., strict OpenSpec validate, diff check, and temp jq acceptance.

Added commitSetupFiles helper to cmd/rally/main.go. After rally init, stages .rally/.gitignore, .rally/config.toml, .rally/README.md and commits as 'rally: initialize workspace'. After laps.InstallHooks, stages .laps/hooks.json and hook scripts and commits as 'rally: install laps hooks'. Both use --no-verify, skip when nothing staged, never sweep user changes, and warn gracefully on git failures. go build and go test pass. Smoke-tested: first init creates exactly one setup commit, second is no-op, dirty working tree files are not swept.

Added 5 tests for commitSetupFiles in cmd/rally/main_test.go covering: init creates exactly one 'rally: initialize workspace' commit, init rerun is no-op, dirty working tree user files are not swept, hook install creates exactly one 'rally: install laps hooks' commit, and hook reinstall is no-op. All use real git repos via gitx helpers. Concurrent-rally duplicate-commit coverage is best-effort — the sequential tests prove idempotency for single-process flows; a true concurrent race would need goroutine-based stress testing with serialization points that are inherently non-deterministic.

Added lap-boundary commit instructions to both hook scripts. laps-done-hook.sh now instructs agent to commit with '<lap-description>: done'. laps-handoff-hook.sh now instructs agent to commit with '<lap-description>: in progress (handoff)'. Both include guidance to replace the placeholder. Added assertions in hooks_test.go for commit message forms and placeholder guidance. All go test ./internal/laps/... tests pass.

Leftover-work commit guidance at run start: dirty non-rally tree injects advisory commit-first prompt via BuildPromptOptions.LeftoverWork + embedded leftover_work.md snippet; tolerates IsWorkspaceDirty errors. Also extended IsWorkspaceDirty to exclude .laps/ (rally's own queue/hook machinery) so guidance doesn't spuriously fire every run. Tests added for dirty/clean/only-rally cases; full suite green.

Verified phases 1-2 of git-hygiene: all 11 targeted tasks (1.1-1.4, 2.1-2.5, 4.3, 4.4) pass deep review. Setup auto-commit uses --no-verify, staged-set guard, and path-scoped git add/commit (no -A). Hook scripts emit exact message forms with placeholder guidance. Leftover-work guidance toggles on IsWorkspaceDirty (now also excludes .laps/), threaded via BuildPromptOptions.LeftoverWork -> embedded leftover_work.md. All 11 tests pass. No substantive gaps found; no follow-up laps needed. Tasks 3.x, 4.1-4.2, 4.5-4.7 remain for later phases.

Phase 3 git-hygiene: retired CommitRallyState/rallyTrackedStatePaths/pathIsGitIgnored; added gitx.FoldRallyState (insurance fold for no-code runs — stages only .rally/.laps, amends rally-prefixed HEAD with [+state] preserving authorship or creates single 'rally: update state', no stacking); rewired runner.go:1133 call site; removed obsolete tests. go build/vet/test green, grep clean.

Phase 3 task 3.5: added 5 tests in internal/gitx/git_test.go covering FoldRallyState's finalization commit behavior over temp git repos — code-run (one work commit folds summary.jsonl, no separate state commit), no-code amend of rally-prefixed HEAD (+[+state] once, authorship preserved, no stacking), no-code non-rally HEAD (single 'rally: update state', second fold amends not stacks), no-changes no-op, and non-git no-op. Drove the real finalization helper (runner.go:1137 calls FoldRallyState); modeled autoCommit's 'git add -A' work commit via a commitAll helper. go test ./internal/relay/... ./internal/gitx/... and go vet pass.

Phase 3 verified: all 5 tasks (3.1-3.5) confirmed complete and correct. FoldRallyState replaces CommitRallyState; autoCommit folds summary.jsonl into work commit; amend-fallback handles no-code runs. Zero dangling references. 5/5 tests pass + smoke confirmed. go build/vet clean. No gaps — no follow-up laps.

Added 15 edge-case hardening tests covering special characters in commit messages (quotes, newlines, dollar signs, backticks) across gitx, cmd/rally, and laps hook scripts; plus git-unavailable/corrupted scenarios (non-git dir, empty .git). All tests pass, go vet clean. No production bugs found — exec.Command passes args directly to git without shell interpolation.

Documented git commit conventions in README.md (setup auto-commits, lap-boundary agent commits, state folding, leftover-work guidance) and AGENTS.md. Bumped VERSION 0.8.3 -> 0.8.4. go build/vet clean.

Final whole-change verification: all 21 tasks verified complete (checked off 4.1-4.2, 4.5-4.7). All 4 spec requirements satisfied with full scenario coverage. go build/test/vet clean. One WARNING flagged: lap-boundary agent commits create timing tension with FoldRallyState (agent commits before rally folds state, producing rally: update state commits). Two minor SUGGESTIONS: clean up legacy .rally/.gitignore entries, and add integration test for pre-committed-agent + FoldRallyState flow. None block archive.
Width-aware ShortcutHint with four tiers (full/medium/narrow/minimal) using term.GetSize, widest-that-fits selection, and comprehensive tests.

Replaced fixed 40-char separators with terminal-width-aware fill capped at 80 in RenderHeader, RenderFooter, and RenderSummary. Added label truncation for narrow terminals. Non-TTY defaults to 80. All 22 tests pass.
Clamped lastActivity to try elapsed time in Tick(), preventing stale previous-try mtime from showing false ages and spurious ⚠ slowing at retry start. Added 3 new tests, updated 3 existing tests.

Retry footer cadence and colouring implemented: interim retries show one dim updating line in-place, terminal outcomes get coloured ✓/✗ block. Fixed cadence tests to accept 'all agents frozen' relay exit. All tests pass (go test ./internal/style/ ./internal/relay/).

Verified §1-§6 display/retry slice: width-aware tiered ShortcutHint, full-width 80-cap headers, try-runtime-bounded activity age (stall detector untouched), collapsed single-line interim retry + terminal-only colouring. All 16 tasks in §1, §2, §5, §6 checked off. No issues found; go test ./internal/style/ ./internal/monitor/ ./internal/relay/ passes.

Snapshot dirty workspace paths at try start alongside headBefore; incomplete classification now uses forward delta (paths newly dirty or with changed porcelain state vs snapshot) instead of absolute dirty set. Untouched inherited leftovers no longer trigger incomplete. Added WorkspaceDirtyPaths to gitx. Four regression tests cover: no-op inheriting leftovers (not incomplete), own unfinalized changes (incomplete), staging inherited leftover (incomplete), no-change-no-finalize (agent error). Updated TestProbationIncompletePromotesToActive to write unique files per attempt. go test ./internal/relay/ ./internal/gitx/ passes.

Verified §7 incomplete-classification: snapshot/delta correct, resilience exemption intact, retry guidance intact. Fixed auto-commit guard (now requires && finalized to prevent inherited dirt from being auto-committed on no-op tries). All 11 incomplete-aware tests pass. Checked off tasks.md §7.1-7.3. Minor: reverse-delta for vanished paths not implemented (revert case edge), but forward delta covers staging, and commits are handled by commitHistory branch.

Added rally init subcommands: init roles (role-only config), init all (workspace + roles). Refactored runInitRoles into runRolesSetup helper. Updated backward-compat init-roles alias to call runInitAll. All subcommands idempotent. 5 new tests covering: init all runs both in sequence, init roles only touches role config, idempotent reruns for both. All 18 init-related tests pass.

Renamed FallbackConfig to FreeRunConfig with full backward compatibility. Config key [free_run] prompt_file replaces [fallback] instructions_file. Legacy [fallback] still loads with deprecation warning. Type alias FallbackConfig=FreeRunConfig, runner fields renamed (loadFreeRunPrompt, builtInDefaultFreeRunPrompt, FreeRunPromptFile). 3 new config tests cover: legacy alias with warning, new key preferred over legacy, absent key. All config and relay tests pass.

Documented init subcommands (init, init roles, init all) with table in README Commands section; documented [free_run] prompt_file key replacing deprecated [fallback] instructions_file with deprecation note; updated config example to use [free_run]; bumped VERSION from 0.8.4 to 0.8.5. All tests pass.

Unified subprocess cancel + stall-kill on SIGINT-to-process-group: SetProcessGroup wires Cmd.Cancel=SIGINT to -pid and Cmd.WaitDelay=5s; freeze_unix maps signalTerminate to SIGINT. Added exec_unix_test pinning group-reach (non-leader child gets SIGINT) then SIGKILL after WaitDelay. Fixed prior incomplete run's deadlock-detector abort in the leader helper (select{} -> time.Sleep loop). build + agent/reliability tests green; tasks.md untouched.

Verified graceful-shutdown slice against dev/bb3658e: tasks 1.1-1.4 checked off and go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/reliability/... passed, but os/exec WaitDelay only SIGKILLs cmd.Process so task 1.5 remains open; queued follow-up implementation and verify laps.

Re-verified graceful-shutdown against dev/bb3658e: Cmd.Cancel sends SIGINT to -pid, WaitDelay is set, freeze_unix maps terminate to SIGINT, and every executor still routes through SetProcessGroup, but Go's os/exec timeout path still escalates with Process.Kill() on the leader only, so task 1.5 remains open; focused tests passed.

Cancel path now SIGKILLs the whole process group after the grace window: SetProcessGroup's Cmd.Cancel escalates group-wide via a new shared reliability.KillProcessGroup (SIGINT then SIGKILL after GracefulShutdownGrace drain) instead of relying on Go's leader-only WaitDelay; ESRCH/already-exited groups resolve as nil (no spurious cancel error). Added linux regression test where both leader and an inherited non-leader child ignore SIGINT, deterministically reaped via subreaper+Wait4 asserting both SIGKILLed; kept the existing group-reach SIGINT assertion. Verified the new test fails on old leader-only behavior. go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/reliability/... green; tasks.md untouched.

Confirmed group-wide cancel escalation: Cmd.Cancel now SIGINTs/SIGKILLs the process group, stubborn-child + group-reach regressions passed, go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/reliability/... passed, and task 1.5 is checked off.

Split shared ActionStop/ActionQuit branch in runner action loop: Ctrl+C (quit-now) sets stopFlag, cancelAttempt(), drains tryCh while staying responsive (second Ctrl+C escalates via new reliability.ForceKillProcessGroup immediate group SIGKILL), then breaks actionLoop so the relay aborts; Ctrl+X (stop) unchanged. Added monitor 'stopping…' indicator (priority over stalled) so the cancel drain never looks frozen. go build ./... green; relay/monitor/reliability/keyboard/agent tests pass; tasks.md untouched.

Extracted runOne's in-try select state machine into testable Runner.runActionLoop and added deterministic tests (fake channels + simulated keyboard.Action) pinning all four acceptance behaviors: Ctrl+C cancels+aborts without waiting, Ctrl+X lets the try finish then stops, a stalled attempt quits promptly without waiting the stall threshold, and a second Ctrl+C during drain escalates via an injectable force-kill hook. No real sleeps near WaitDelay; go test ./internal/relay/... green (incl. -race), vet clean, tasks.md untouched.

Verified stop/quit against dev/bb3658e: Ctrl+C now cancels, drains, and aborts; Ctrl+X still stops after the current try; second quit stays responsive; tasks 2.1-2.4 checked off; focused relay/agent tests passed; full ./internal/relay/... -race still hits a pre-existing store test race outside this diff.

Re-verified stop/quit against dev/bb3658e: Ctrl+C cancels, drains, and aborts; Ctrl+X still stops after the current try; second quit stays responsive; stopping indicator is wired; focused relay/monitor/agent tests passed; ./internal/relay/... -race still hits the pre-existing store race in TestE2E_WindowsFreezeDisabledRetryBudgetExhaustion outside this diff.

Re-verified agent-lifecycle stop/quit against dev/bb3658e: ActionQuit cancels, drains, and aborts; ActionStop still waits for the current try; second quit stays responsive via force-kill; the stopping indicator is wired; targeted relay/monitor/agent tests and targeted race runs passed; full ./internal/relay/... -race still only hits the pre-existing store race in TestE2E_WindowsFreezeDisabledRetryBudgetExhaustion.

Verified agent-lifecycle stop/quit against dev: ActionQuit cancels, drains, and aborts; ActionStop still waits; second quit force-kills during drain; stopping indicator is wired; targeted scenario tests and targeted race runs passed; full internal/relay race still only hits the pre-existing store race in TestE2E_WindowsFreezeDisabledRetryBudgetExhaustion.

Re-verified agent-lifecycle stop/quit against dev/bb3658e without consuming the unrelated resume head task: ActionQuit cancels, drains, and aborts; ActionStop still stops after the current try; second quit escalates via force-kill; stopping indicator is wired; targeted relay/monitor/agent scenario tests passed; full ./internal/relay/... -race still only hits the pre-existing store race in TestE2E_WindowsFreezeDisabledRetryBudgetExhaustion.

Verified honest resume wiring: opencode --session flag wired, gemini ResumeSupported=false, runner policy preserved (resume-on-retry + FreshRestart + degrade-to-fresh). Tasks 3.1-3.4 checked off. Added 3 follow-up laps: codex resume wiring (work-ae43), contract+regression tests (work-98a8), verify follow-up (work-0898).

Added contract tests (task 3.5) verifying resume flags in CLI args for claude (--resume), antigravity (--conversation=), opencode (--session) executors when ResumeSessionID is set. Added regression tests (task 3.6) for pause-captures-session-ID, resume-reuses-on-next-attempt, and explicit-skip-starts-fresh flows at both action-loop and runner levels. Codex deferred to work-ae43. All tests pass.

Verified codex resume wiring + contract tests: (1) all ResumeSupported()==true harnesses pass resume flag — claude (--resume), agy (--conversation=), opencode (--session), and codex (resume <id>, was missing — fixed in fe983ea). (2) Contract tests now cover all four resume-supporting harnesses. (3) Regression tests cover pause-capture, resume-reuse, skip-fresh, FreshRestart-fresh. (4) Smoke: claude and codex real-backend tests pass after fix. (5) Pre-existing resilience retry-budget test failure is unrelated.
Created opencode-e2e.txt with 'opencode e2e pass'

Verified honest resume wiring (3.1-3.7): all 5 executors correctly wired (claude --resume ✅, agy --conversation= ✅, opencode --session ✅, codex resume <id> ✅, gemini ResumeSupported=false ✅). Runner policy preserved: resume-on-retry + FreshRestart clears + no meaningful-progress heuristic. Contract tests cover all 4 resume-supporting harnesses (all pass; codex test flaky from pre-existing pipe race in mock infra). Regression tests pass (pause-capture, resume-reuse, skip-fresh, FreshRestart-fresh). Smoke: real-backend claude + codex relays pass. Tasks.md 3.1-3.7 checked off.
Renamed tier 0 shortcut labels: Ctrl+X 'graceful stop', Ctrl+C 'quit now'. Tiers 1-3 unchanged. All style tests pass.

Implemented single-runner lane warning (tasks 5.1, 5.2, 5.4). Detects lanes with exactly one runner entry in newResolvedRouteRuntime, emits warning to stderr at relay start via routeRuntime.Warnings(). Added multi-runner lane encouragement to README routes section and .rally/config.toml. Tests: single-entry lane warns, multi-entry does not, override lane warns, mixed lanes warn only the single-runner lane. All relay tests pass.

Reconciled VERIFY role default-boundary spec to doc (tasks 6.1-6.4). verify.md and spec are already consistent: reporting-focused, trivial fixes allowed, substantial gaps→head lap, OpenSpec-agnostic. No tasks.md references in rally core. prepare-laps correctly injects per-lap for OpenSpec laps only. AGENTS.md boundary rules aligned. go build green. No code changes needed.

Verified tasks 4.x/5.x/6.x against merge target feat/harden-relay-run-lifecycle: (4.x) shortcutHintTiers with 'graceful stop'/'quit now' in tier 0, all tiers fit width without overflow; (5.x) single-runner warning fires once at relay start via stderr for one-entry lanes, silent for multi-entry; (6.x) verify.md is reporting-focused with small-fix allowance, OpenSpec-agnostic, AGENTS.md boundary rules aligned. go test ./internal/style/... ./internal/relay/... passes. No gaps found.

Documented lifecycle behavior in README: graceful stop vs quit now timing, pause/resume, double-press confirm window (4s), single-runner-lane warning. Updated Quick start shortcut table with Ctrl+X/C/P/S. Bumped VERSION to 0.8.6. go build green.

Final full-change verification: go build + tests pass (only pre-existing flaky TestCodexExecutor_ResumeFlagInArgs). Verified: cancel→SIGINT→group→SIGKILL, Ctrl+C immediate/Ctrl+X after-try, ResumeSupported() contract tests pass (codex/opencode wired, gemini=false), labels 'graceful stop'/'quit now', single-runner lane warning, verify.md consistent, tasks.md 7.1-7.4 checked off, VERSION 0.8.6. All tests pass including exhaustive mapping coverage, load-bearing invariant that usage_limit/invalid_model/auth_or_proxy are NOT FailureInfra, and display label harness-name checks.

Reordered ClassifyError to 5-tier priority (Evidence > provider/quota > dirty-tree incomplete > harness-scoped patterns > agent_error). Added Harness and Category fields to Pattern; scoped claude rate-limit, opencode, antigravity, codex patterns to their harnesses. Assigned FailureCategory to all ErrorPatterns entries (transient_infra for timeout/network/TLS/5xx, harness_launch for exec errors, short_rate_limit for rate limits, usage_limit for quota). Runner.go call site threads picked.Harness. Nil/empty Evidence tolerated. All tests pass including acceptance test that Codex 'rate-limit' prose does NOT match Claude rate limit.

Verified phase 1-2 against main from pre-batch ca03256; checked off 1.1-1.3, 1.5, 2.1, 2.3-2.5, and queued work-dd36/work-1e8f after finding evidence is not wired through runner.go and usage_limit still stays infra on the text-pattern path.

This seems like an empty workspace. Let me find the actual project directory with the Go source code.
Found it at `/workspace`. Let me explore the project structure and read the key files.
Now I can see the two issues clearly. Let me also look at the `FailureEvidence` type and `CategoryToClass` function, and the `TryResult.Evidence` field:
Now let me look at the `agent.TryResult` struct to see the Evidence field, and the area around the runner's result handling:
Now I have a clear understanding of the two bugs:

**Bug 1** (line 1272 of runner.go): `ClassifyError` is called without passing `result.Evidence`, so executor evidence is never used in the real runner path.

**Bug 2** (lines 372-377 of patterns.go): When a text pattern matches, the `FailureClass` comes directly from `pattern.FailureClass` rather than going through `CategoryToClass()`. This means the `usage_limit` pattern with `FailureClass: FailureInfra` bypasses the `CategoryToClass` mapping which maps `usage_limit → FailureAgent`.

Let me now look at the test file and laps configuration:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me also look at the `result` variable near line 1272 to confirm how to wire evidence:

Wired result.Evidence into runner ClassifyError call and made CategoryToClass authoritative for categorized text-pattern classes; usage_limit no longer feeds the freeze counter. Added reliability + runner regression tests; relay & reliability packages green.
Re-verified tasks 1.4/2.2, added runner regressions for evidence priority and non-infra category mapping, and checked the boxes after targeted tests passed.

Added ParseGeminiError and ParseClaudeError helpers in internal/reliability/ that parse Antigravity/Gemini and Claude provider-specific error signatures into populated FailureEvidence structs. Antigravity/Gemini: RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, Individual quota reached, Resets in <dur> (day-aware duration parsing). Claude: rate_limit_event (five-hour vs seven-day), model_not_found, authentication_failed, 529 overload. Table tests per signature asserting resolved category and parsed timing fields. go test ./internal/reliability/... green.

Added ParseCodexError (usage-limit with try-again-at timing, auth, model-not-found, rate-limit) and ParseOpencodeError (JSON error events mapped to categories with provider extraction from model name). Table tests per signature, all green.

Wired reliability parsers (ParseGeminiError, ParseClaudeError, ParseCodexError, ParseOpencodeError) into all 5 per-harness executor adapters. On error paths, adapters now return TryResult with populated FailureEvidence when structured error signatures are detected; unknown errors fall through to (nil, error) for runner-side fallback. Added 8 adapter tests (one representative signature per harness plus negative cases for Claude and Gemini). go build ./... and go test ./internal/{agent,reliability,relay}/... all green.
